q: Atoms of element are rarely found in nature.
Everything around us is made up of elements, molecules, or compounds.
All the inorganic and organic materials are not created with chemical bonds.
The outermost shell of electrons are called valence electrons.
The s-subshell has only one spherical-shaped orbital.
The p-subshell has five dumbbell-shaped orbitals.
The d-subshell has five dumbbell-shaped orbitals.
The electrons are filled in the lower energy atomic orbitals after filling higher energy ones.
Hund’s rule states that an electron occupies the lowest energy orbital first.
The two electrons with the same spin can occupy the same orbital.
According to Pauli's principle, all the electrons in singly occupied orbitals have the same spin.
Hund’s rule states that every orbital in a sub-shell is singly occupied before any orbital is doubly occupied.
According to Pauli's exclusion principle the two electrons in the same orbital will have parallel spins.
According to Pauli's exclusion principle, no more two electrons in an atom can have same orbital.
The electrons in the valence shells of an atom are known as the valence.
Carbon is in Group VI and has 6 valence electrons.
Atoms can only gain electrons to obtain an octet.
Atoms will lose, gain, or share electrons to obtain an octet.
The valence electrons of elements represented using Lewis symbols are in s orbitals only.
Chemical bond is formed only when by sharing the valence electrons between the atoms involved in the bond.
Electronegativity is the ability of an atom to attract electrons.
The amount of energy released when an electron is added to a gaseous atom.
The amount of energy required to add an electron from a gaseous atom.
The elements having high electron affinity easily lose electrons.
The higher the value of the electronegativity, the greater is the atom’s attraction to electrons.
The elements having high ionisation energy results in the formation of the cations.
There are two main types of chemical bonding.
The intermolecular forces are strong forces.
An ionic bond is stronger than a covalent bond.
Some compounds have both ionic and covalent character.
The octet rule can apply to d-block elements.
An ionic bond involves electron sharing between two atoms.
The electrons transfer from one atom having large ionisation energy to another atom having small electron affinity.
Ionic bonding depends on the difference in electronegativity between two atoms.
A metal loses electrons to form positively charged ions (cations).
The electronegativity difference value is less than 1.8 for an ionic bond.
An ionic bond is the attractive force between a positive ion and a negative ion.
The valency of an element is the number of electrons lost only by an atom to form an ionic bond.
Mg2+ and O2- ions have the electronic configuration of stable noble gas of Ar.
In ionic compounds, the cations and anions are held together by strong electrostatic attraction.
The greater the charge on the ions, the greater the electrostatic attraction and the stronger the ionic bond will be.
The melting points of the ionic compounds with the smaller charge on the ions are higher.
In solid ionic compounds, their ions are held in variable positions and cannot move.
Solid ionic compounds can conduct electricity.
Coordination number is the number of atoms or ions surrounding a central atom in a crystal lattice.
Covalent bonding can occur between identical atoms only.
In a covalent bond there are complete transfer of electrons between two atoms.
The covalent bond is the electrostatic attraction between the nuclei of the atoms and the transferred electrons.
Covalent bonding usually occurs between non-metals.
The length of the bond is determined by the number of non-bonded electrons.
In single covalent bonds, only two pair of electrons is shared between two atoms.
The higher the number of bonded electrons, the weaker the attraction between the two atoms.
The higher the number of bonded electrons, the longer the bond length.
The bond strength of the triple bond is the highest.
The single bonds are the longest and stronger than double and triple bonds.
CO molecule has a triple bond that consists of two covalent bonds and one coordinate bond.
A coordinate bond is a special type of covalent bond.
H2O molecule is an example of the formation of dative bond.
A Lewis structure is only three-dimensional representation of a molecule.
In a graphite, each carbon atom is joined to four other carbon atoms by covalent bonds.
The fullerene (C60), carbon nanotubes, and graphene are giant molecules.
The fullerene (C60), carbon nanotubes, and graphene are bulky covalent molecules.
Silicon(IV) oxide, SiO2 (silica or quartz) is a giant covalent molecule.
Giant structure is a three-dimensional structure of atoms that are joined by a network of bonds.
Graphite and silica have layered structures.
Diamond and graphite contain covalent bonds between carbon atoms.
Silicon dioxide conducts electricity because it contains free electrons.
Silicon dioxide is a giant covalent molecule with four oxygen atoms bonded to each silicon atom.
Graphite conducts electricity because it contains free electrons.
The structure of silicon dioxide is similar to graphite.
Graphite contains unbonded electrons which move through the structure.
The force of attraction between the constituent particles in any form of matter is the intermolecular force.
VSEPR theory is a theory to predict the three-dimensional shapes of molecules.
The covalent bonds can be polar or non-polar.
The water molecule is a non-polar molecule.
CO2 molecule has no dipole moment since C-O bonds are nonpolar.
Hydrogen bonding can occur in intermolecular forces only.
The end of partial positive charge of a dipole is the atom with the highest electronegativity.
HCN is a linear molecule, therefore it is a nonpolar molecule.
The shape of BF3 is a trigonal planar geometry, so it is considered nonpolar.
When electrons are not shared equally between two atoms, a polar covalent bond occurs.
Ion-dipole interactions are formed between a metal and a non-metal.
London dispersion forces can be formed between non-polar molecules.
The hydrogen bond is stronger than a covalent bond.
Hydrogen bonds involve hydrogen bonded to carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, or fluorine.
Hydrogen bonding can occur in liquid water.
A hydrogen bond is a special type of dipole-dipole interaction.
van der Waals forces are strong intermolecular forces that bind molecules together.
London dispersion forces are the strongest in intermolecular forces.
All metals are solids at room temperature.
In a metallic bond, the atoms do share or transfer electrons to bond together.
The structure of a metallic bond is the same from that of covalent or ionic bonds.
Metals conduct electricity when solid because positive ions are free to move.
The positively charged metallic nuclei are surrounded by free-moving electrons.
Metals consist of giant structures of atoms.
The strength of metallic bond is higher than that of an ionic bond.
The strength of metallic bonding increases with the decreasing positive charge on the ions in the metal lattice.
Metals conduct electricity when molten because negative ions are free to move.
Metallic bond is a strong force of attraction between the metal ions and the electrons.
